name: Key and Lock
description: Key and Lock is a rule-based challenge that asks the player to observe, reason, manipulate state, or sequence actions.
design_notes: Key and Lock should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For puzzle mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use key and lock as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect key and lock to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels clever when they infer the rule and manipulate the system correctly.

parameters.name: activation_condition
parameters.description: Rule that determines when the puzzle element changes state.

parameters.name: reset_time
parameters.description: Delay before the puzzle returns to its default state.

parameters.name: feedback_intensity
parameters.description: How strongly the game communicates success, failure, or partial progress.

edge_cases: Key and Lock is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Key and Lock overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Key and Lock runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Key and Lock state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Key and Lock plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Key and Lock produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Key and Lock strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Key and Lock becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Key and Lock with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Key and Lock as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Key and Lock with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Key and Lock as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
